title: batrachium rhipiphyllum
intro: Batrachium rhipiphyllum, also known as the aquatic liverwort, is a type of non-vascular, thalloid plant that belongs to the family Scapaniaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Batrachium rhipiphyllum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
